description: CI-safe broker canary payload for Round 4 rollout preparation — does not touch live broker or services. Operator can validate handler-to-runner conversion and evidence contract without Docker or GitHub mutations.

canary.title: CI-safe broker canary payload for Round 4 rollout
canary.focus: Validate handler-to-runner conversion and evidence contract with a synthetic payload that does not touch live broker, Docker, or GitHub.
canary.acceptance: buildRunnerTaskFromHandlerPayload conversion test; rollout docs evidence(PR/Done/Block) guide; npm check/build/lint/test pass
canary.prompt: This is a CI-safe canary payload. Do not execute real work — validate conversion only.

evidenceGuide.prUrl: Runner emitted a PR URL. Operator confirms the worker can create and push branches, commit changes, and open pull requests through the coding-agent contract.
evidenceGuide.doneCommentUrl: Runner posted a Done comment on the issue. Used for tasks that complete without creating a PR (e.g., verification-only tasks).
evidenceGuide.blockCommentUrl: Runner posted a Block comment. Indicates the task is impossible or unsafe to complete in its current form; operator should inspect the block reason before proceeding.
evidenceGuide.noEvidence: Runner completed without any structured evidence. This is a degraded state — the handler returns status=blocked with a risks entry. Operator should investigate the coding-agent output.

operatorChecklist: 3. Verify npm check/build/lint/test all pass on the deployed commit
4. For each active target (bangtong, dungae, sogyo, nosuk): run doctor, one smoke task, and verify evidence fields
5. yukson is explicitly excluded — do not validate, restart, or deploy to legacy yukson/VPS2

excludedLegacy.reason: yukson / VPS2 legacy worker is explicitly out of scope for Round 4 rollout
excludedLegacy.action: Do not change, restart, or validate legacy Yukson/VPS2 worker services
